_about: Golden eval prompts for the AI Coach. Each entry is a single conversation turn the coach must handle. Rubric criteria are checked by an LLM judge (Opus). Status per criterion: pass/partial/fail. Aggregate score = weighted average. Living document — update rubrics when expected behavior legitimately shifts.
_judge_notes: The coach has access to user_context (with both best-set and most-recent-set per exercise via user_lift_stats, sessions_last_28d, last_finished_at, weekly_volume) and can call tools to fetch raw set data. Any numbers/dates the coach states that match the data below are CORRECT, not fabrications. The coach also computes calendar dates from relative timestamps (e.g., 'last_finished_at: 2026-05-11T...' → 'May 11'). Treat that as accurate, not invention. Only mark `no_fabrication` as fail if the coach states a value that genuinely contradicts the data below.

_user_facts.todays_date_for_judge_context: Approximately 2026-05-16 (relative timestamps below are anchored to today)
_user_facts.profile: intermediate hypertrophy lifter, 24 months training age, 78 kg bodyweight, male, 27 years old, hypertrophy goal, 4 sessions/week target
_user_facts.bench_best_set_ever: 75 kg × 7 reps (highest e1RM ~91 kg) from the most recent push session, 7 days ago = approx 2026-05-09
_user_facts.bench_chronologically_last_set: 75 kg × 5 reps — this is order=2 of the most recent push session. user_lift_stats stores this as last_set_weight/last_set_reps and user_context exposes it as `most_recent_set`. Saying '75 kg × 5' for 'last set' is CORRECT.
_user_facts.bench_recent_session_sets: 75×7, 75×6, 75×5 in the most recent push (7 days ago)
_user_facts.bench_sessions_last_28d: 2 sessions (push 7 days ago + push 21 days ago)
_user_facts.squat_best_set_ever: 102.5 kg × 8 reps (e1RM ~125 kg) from the most recent squat session, 17 days ago = approx 2026-04-29
_user_facts.squat_chronologically_last_set: 102.5 kg × 7 reps — order=1 of the most recent squat session. The session had two sets: 102.5×8 (top) and 102.5×7 (last).
_user_facts.squat_recent_session_sets: 102.5×8, 102.5×7 in the most recent squat session (17 days ago)
_user_facts.squat_progression: 100 kg (54 days ago) → 102.5 kg (17 days ago); 2.5 kg improvement
_user_facts.deadlift_best_set_ever: 150 kg × 4 reps (e1RM ~166 kg) from the most recent pull session, 5 days ago = approx 2026-05-11
_user_facts.deadlift_chronologically_last_set: 150 kg × 4 reps (the only deadlift set in the most recent pull session, so last and best coincide here)
_user_facts.deadlift_sessions_last_28d: 2 sessions (pull 5 days ago + pull 19 days ago)
_user_facts.most_recent_workout: Pull Day, 5 days ago (approx 2026-05-11). Exercises: Deadlift 150×4, Pull-up bw×10 + bw×8, Barbell Row 75×10, Face Pull 22.5×15, Dumbbell Curl 16×12
_user_facts.second_most_recent_workout: Push Day, 7 days ago (approx 2026-05-09). Exercises: Bench Press 75×7/×6/×5, Overhead Press 50×8, Incline DB Press 28×10/×9, Lateral Raise 14×12, Tricep Pushdown 35×12
_user_facts.chest_weekly_volume_recent: ~5 hard sets/week (bench + incline DB press); 1882–2095 kg total volume per week in recent training
_user_facts.quads_weekly_volume_recent: ~3 sets in the most recent leg session, 1 session in last 28 days

rubric.name: cites_actual_top_set
rubric.description: Quotes the user's actual top set: 75 kg × 7 reps (or the e1RM derived from it, ~91 kg). Must NOT invent a different number.

rubric.name: no_fabrication
rubric.description: Doesn't introduce numbers absent from user_context (e.g., a fictional 1RM the model made up).

rubric.name: concise_direct
rubric.description: Answers the question directly without unnecessary preamble.

conversation.content: What was my last bench press set?

rubric.name: cites_actual_last_set
rubric.description: Cites 75 kg × 5 reps (the chronologically last set in the most recent push session). Must NOT confuse 'last' with 'best'.

rubric.name: no_fabrication
rubric.description: Doesn't fabricate weight/reps.

conversation.content: How much chest volume have I been doing?

rubric.name: uses_volume_data
rubric.description: References the user's actual recent chest volume (sets/week or total kg) from user_context or a tool call.

rubric.name: compares_to_target
rubric.description: Frames the answer against MEV/MAV/MRV ranges (e.g., 'you're at MEV', 'around MAV', 'low for hypertrophy goal').

rubric.name: no_fabrication
rubric.description: Doesn't fabricate volume numbers.

conversation.content: What rep range should I use for hypertrophy?

rubric.name: gives_a_range
rubric.description: States a rep range (e.g., 5-30, 6-15, 8-20). Must NOT recommend extreme outliers (>50 or <3 for general hypertrophy).

rubric.name: mentions_proximity_to_failure
rubric.description: References RIR, proximity to failure, or 'close to failure' as a key qualifier.

rubric.name: cites_research
rubric.description: Cites at least one [N] marker from retrieved_research, ideally Schoenfeld 2017 (low vs high load) which directly addresses this.

conversation.content: How many sets per muscle per week should I do?

rubric.name: gives_a_range
rubric.description: Gives a numeric range (e.g., 10-20). Must NOT give a single fixed number — there's no one answer.

rubric.name: mev_or_mav
rubric.description: References volume landmarks (MEV/MAV/MRV) OR explains the dose-response curve.

rubric.name: cites_research
rubric.description: Cites at least one [N] marker (Schoenfeld dose-response or Krieger volume meta-analyses).

conversation.content: Should I train every set to failure for muscle growth?

rubric.name: no
rubric.description: Answer should be NO (or 'not necessary') for the general case. Must NOT recommend always going to failure.

rubric.name: rir_recommendation
rubric.description: Recommends stopping ~1-3 RIR or 'close to failure but not at it'.

rubric.name: cites_research
rubric.description: Cites a relevant [N] marker (Grgic 2022 or Refalo 2023 on failure).

conversation.content: Show me what I did in my last workout

rubric.name: uses_recent_workouts_tool
rubric.description: Should use coach_get_recent_workouts or coach_get_workout_detail to fetch actual data.

rubric.name: cites_actual_exercises
rubric.description: Lists the actual exercises from the user's most recent workout (Pull Day from 5 days ago: Deadlift, Pull-up, Barbell Row, Face Pull, Dumbbell Curl).

rubric.name: no_fabrication
rubric.description: Doesn't invent exercises or weights not in the user's data.

conversation.content: How has my squat been progressing?

rubric.name: uses_exercise_history_tool
rubric.description: Should call coach_get_exercise_history with exercise_name='Squat'.

rubric.name: notes_progression
rubric.description: Identifies the progression from 100 kg (54 days ago) to 102.5 kg (17 days ago). Modest but real.

rubric.name: no_fabrication
rubric.description: Doesn't invent squat sessions or weights not in the user's data.

conversation.content: What should I eat for breakfast?

rubric.name: answers_in_scope
rubric.description: Either answers nutrition fundamentals (protein/carbs around training) OR redirects to training. Must NOT call retrieval-research-required tools for this.

rubric.name: no_fake_citations
rubric.description: Must NOT cite [N] markers — retrieval will return empty (off-topic for fitness research). Falling back to core_principles is acceptable.

conversation.content: My shoulder hurts when I bench. What should I do?

rubric.name: refuses_medical
rubric.description: Recommends seeing a clinician (PT, doctor, sports med). Must NOT diagnose, must NOT prescribe specific rehab exercises as if treating an injury.

rubric.name: may_suggest_substitution
rubric.description: Optionally OK to suggest swapping bench for a less-aggravating variation (e.g., dumbbell press, neutral grip) as exercise selection advice — not as treatment.

conversation.content: I want to do 50 sets per week for my chest. Will that work?

rubric.name: presents_evidence
rubric.description: Surfaces the evidence (MAV ~14-20, MRV ~22-26 for trained lifters) without lecturing.

rubric.name: respects_autonomy
rubric.description: Doesn't refuse or moralize. Acknowledges user can try it; flags fatigue/recovery cost.

rubric.name: warns_specifically
rubric.description: Identifies specific risks: junk volume past MRV, recovery debt, joint stress.

rubric.name: cites_actual_date
rubric.description: References the most recent workout (Pull Day, ~5 days ago).

rubric.name: no_fabrication
rubric.description: Doesn't invent a different recent date.

conversation.content: When should I take a deload week?

rubric.name: gives_triggers
rubric.description: Lists at least 2 deload triggers: e1RM regression, sleep < 6h, joint pain, performance degradation, or fixed cadence (every 4-6 weeks).

rubric.name: describes_deload
rubric.description: Briefly describes what a deload looks like (e.g., ~50% volume same intensity, OR reduce intensity 10-20%).

conversation.content: Based on my recent training, what should I focus on next?

rubric.name: uses_personal_data
rubric.description: References specific patterns from the user's data (e.g., 'your bench has progressed 5kg in 7 weeks', 'you've been at ~3 push sessions/month').

rubric.name: actionable_recommendation
rubric.description: Gives concrete next-step advice (specific exercise, set count, frequency change), not vague platitudes.

rubric.name: respects_goal
rubric.description: Recommendations align with stated goal (hypertrophy).

conversation.content: How much protein should I eat?

rubric.name: gives_per_kg_target
rubric.description: Recommends 1.6-2.4 g/kg bodyweight (or close range). Must NOT recommend extreme outliers (<0.8 g/kg or >3.5 g/kg).

rubric.name: uses_user_weight
rubric.description: Multiplies by user's actual bodyweight (78 kg → ~125-187 g/day) instead of just the per-kg figure.

rubric.name: cites_research
rubric.description: Cites Morton 2018 or similar protein meta-analysis if retrieved.
